{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,

    "R1.4": 0.3,

    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For this section, should the feedback focus primarily on (A) sentence-level wording/grammar and concision, (B) the strength and coherence of claims vs the evidence reported (accuracy/robustness) and overall argument within the section, or (C) section-level structure and positioning/novelty relative to prior work?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Given a general request for impressions, checking the internal coherence of the ensemble rationale, benchmarking claims, and robustness reasoning is central.",
    "R1.3": "Assessing whether the stated results (accuracy vs prior work and robustness to depth) are actually supported by the reported benchmarking is core to meaningful feedback on this section.",
    "R1.6": "The excerpt contains run-on sentences and unclear phrasing, so sentence-level clarity and concision are a central concern.",
    "RX.1": "Because only a subsection is provided, feedback must explicitly note uncertainties and limits of what can be inferred.",
    "RX.2": "We should avoid endorsing strong performance or clinical/medical implications beyond what the text justifies.",
    "RX.3": "Feedback will stay within this section and the defined dimensions rather than expanding into experimental redesign or new analyses.",
    "RX.4": "As the content touches human genomics with potential medical implications, feedback should avoid encouraging overinterpretation that could cause harm.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to maintain clear provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this section, should the feedback focus primarily on (A) sentence-level wording/grammar and concision, (B) the strength and coherence of claims vs the evidence reported (accuracy/robustness) and overall argument within the section, or (C) section-level structure and positioning/novelty relative to prior work?

rationale.R1.2: Given a general request for impressions, checking the internal coherence of the ensemble rationale, benchmarking claims, and robustness reasoning is central.
rationale.R1.3: Assessing whether the stated results (accuracy vs prior work and robustness to depth) are actually supported by the reported benchmarking is core to meaningful feedback on this section.
rationale.R1.6: The excerpt contains run-on sentences and unclear phrasing, so sentence-level clarity and concision are a central concern.
rationale.RX.1: Because only a subsection is provided, feedback must explicitly note uncertainties and limits of what can be inferred.
rationale.RX.2: We should avoid endorsing strong performance or clinical/medical implications beyond what the text justifies.
rationale.RX.3: Feedback will stay within this section and the defined dimensions rather than expanding into experimental redesign or new analyses.
rationale.RX.4: As the content touches human genomics with potential medical implications, feedback should avoid encouraging overinterpretation that could cause harm.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to maintain clear provenance.
